finding: The Cloudflare deployment workflow had a contract-alignment audit but no reusable fail-closed audit for trigger scope, exact permissions, production serialization, protected environment binding, or direct secret interpolation in shell commands.

verification.runtime: isolated local Node runtime using byte-identical committed source and test content

advisory.implication: Major-version action tags are mutable references. Exact commit pins would improve supply-chain reproducibility, but pinning was not performed without current verified upstream commit identities.

privacy_boundary: Static public repository workflow source and contract metadata only. No credentials, private chat, health information, or secret values were read or recorded.
limits: No GitHub environment protection rules or secret existence were observable.
Credential validity and Cloudflare authorization remain unproved.
The new audit is not yet wired into the deployment workflow gate.
Static source checks cannot establish action integrity or deployment success.
falsification_route: Wire the audit into CI, then alter one protected property at a time. Any automatic trigger, expanded permission, missing concurrency serialization, wrong environment, or shell-level secret interpolation that still returns security_contract_satisfied falsifies the audit.
